action.content: Compose song lyrics about a socio-economic problem. The song should be in English and in all lowercase letters.
action.label: context

action.content:
the rent is due again
and the landlord called
saying if the check isn't here
he'll take the floorboards right out from under my feet

i work two jobs just to keep the lights on
but the bills stack up like snow in november
snow that melts into debt
debt that never ends

my neighbor lost the car
my sister lost the job
we all live in the same neighborhood
but we don't know each other
just strangers sharing the same silence
in a hallway that smells like stale coffee

the city is bright with neon signs
promising things you can never buy
while the streetlights flicker out
one by one in the dark

i count my change in my pocket
hoping for a miracle
but the system is heavy
and it weighs on the shoulders of the poor
until they can't hold it up

so i close my eyes
and dream of a world
where the rent doesn't rise
where the car stays in the driveway
where the lights stay on
just for a little while longer

before the darkness takes us all
and we're just numbers on a page
in a world too big for hope
in a world too small for lives
action.label: response
